channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Elon Musk's underground digital detective reveals all to NewsNation
item.description: NewsNation's Brian Entin has the first-ever interview with the tech genius using AI to discover government waste and corruption.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Irish leaders boycott Washington St Patrick's Day events over Trump's Gaza comments
item.description: Sinn Féin leader Mary Lou McDonald and Northern Ireland First Minister Michelle O’Neill will skip St. Patrick’s Day in the U.S. this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE, EPA team up to claw back $67 million in 'environmental justice' grants
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency and the Department of Government Efficiency are cutting nearly $70 million in Biden era environmental grants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court turns down chance to claw back abortion clinic buffer zones
item.description: The Supreme Court turned down an opportunity to overturn its precedent permitting buffer zones around abortion clinics over the objections of two of the court’s leading conservatives. In two orders issued Monday, the court declined to take up challenges to ordinances in Carbondale, Ill.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: US-Ukraine rift breaks out at United Nations on 3rd anniversary of the war
item.description: The U.S. is calling on members of the United Nations General Assembly to nix a proposed resolution from Ukraine demanding Russian troops withdrawal from the country, in favor of their own resolution that urges lasting peace but does not compel Russia to leave the war-torn region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court turns back challenges to laws keeping abortion opponents away from clinics, patients
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court refused Monday to hear a pair of cases from abortion opponents who say laws limiting anti-abortion demonstrations near clinics violate their First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump budget bill hits turbulence ahead of House vote as Senate backup looms
item.description: House and Senate Republicans are working to advance dueling versions of their budget reconciliation bills.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE claims it has saved billions, but nearly 75% unaccounted for
item.description: DOGE hasn't provided evidence for approximately $38 billion in alleged savings — nearly 75% of its total claim.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Bureaucracies 'cannibalizing our nation' lawmaker says, expressing empathy for federal workers facing job cuts
item.description: Rep. Clay Higgins expresses empathy for federal workers facing joblessness, but says bureaucracies have been "cannibalizing our nation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alaska can be 'cure' for nation's 'ills' with help from Trump admin, governor says
item.description: Alaska Gov. Mike Dunleavy said in a Fox News interview that the Last Frontier can be first in helping the nation move forward on multiple fronts, including energy independence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: National Cancer Institute spends $218M per year on grants for 'underrepresented' groups: source
item.description: As DOGE continues putting government spending on the chopping block, DO No Harm -- a nonprofit focused on getting DEI out of medicine -- found the National Cancer Institute is spending hundreds of millions of dollars annually to boost minority representation in the cancer workforce.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Millionaire Vivek Ramaswamy set to join the Ohio governor's race
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Vivek Ramaswamy, the Cincinnati-born biotech entrepreneur who departed the Department of Government Efficiency initiative on President Donald Trump's first day, was expected to launch his bid for Ohio governor Monday.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth defends Trump's firings of Pentagon leaders and says there may be more dismissals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth insists President Donald Trump ’s abrupt firing of the nation’s senior military officer amid a wave of dismissals at the Pentagon wasn't unusual, brushing aside outcry that the new administration is openly seeking to inject politics into the military.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Federal workers sue Elon Musk over email ultimatum
item.description: The lawsuit seeks to block mass layoffs, including any connected to an email distributed by the Office of Personnel Management on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: New FBI Director Kash Patel will also be named acting head of the ATF, official says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New FBI Director Kash Patel is expected to be named the acting head of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, a Justice Department official said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch live: Trump set to address CPAC
item.description: President Trump on Saturday afternoon will headline the final day of the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Precious water: Nevada lawmakers have decisions ahead on conservation bills
item.description: Why aren't the rule surrounding groundwater extraction the same for everyone? That's the basic question behind a bill in the Nevada Legislature, one of several proposed laws surrounding water issues this session.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: National Park Service rangers laid off as DOGE makes cuts
item.description: National Park Service ranger Mikayla Moors was one of thousands of government employees laid off by the DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump spars with Maine's governor at the White House over transgender athletes
item.description: President Donald Trump sparred with Maine's Democratic governor during a meeting of governors at the White House on Friday, with Gov. Janet Mills telling the Republican president, “We'll see you in court,” over his push to deny federal funding to the state over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Meet four Minnesota federal workers mourning the loss of their dream jobs
item.description: Many Minnesotans are among those facing unemployment, as the Trump administration slashes jobs across agencies. The state is home to more than 18,000 federal workers, with nearly 60 percent working for the Department of Veterans Affairs and the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: NIH funding freeze stalls applications on $1.5 billion in medical research funds
item.description: The National Institutes of Health had to stop considering new grant applications, delaying funding for research into diseases ranging from heart disease and cancer to Alzheimer’s and allergies.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump DEI executive orders
item.description: U.S. District Judge Adam B. Abelson, a former President Biden appointee, issued a preliminary injunction on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump fires top US general in unprecedented Pentagon shakeup
item.description: A U.S. official said Brown was relieved with immediate effect, even before the Senate confirms his successor.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Dexter's Law: Pushing for tougher sentencing for animal cruelty
item.description: One Tampa Bay area lawmaker is pushing to toughen sentences for animal cruelty, specifically in cases like the shocking discovery of Dexter, a decapitated dog found at Fort DeSoto Beach.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: ‘In Trump We Trust’ Is Exploding
item.description: President Trump forced through his Cabinet – we told you that would only increase his control over the Republican Party, and it did. To that point, Matt Lewis headlines in The Hill, “Conservatism used to mean something. Now it’s just whatever Trump says.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Fired federal workers hunt for new jobs but struggle to replace their old ones
item.description: Job searches can be tough for anyone, but some of the federal workers flooding the market after widespread layoffs are finding it particularly tough.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sedensky, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: After mass layoffs, some federal agencies are trying to bring employees back
item.description: A number of federal agencies, from HHS to the USDA, have laid off employees only to rescind those terminations days later. An expert says it "suggests something about mismanagement of government.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers to Trump: Reverse cuts to 9/11 health care
item.description: New York's senate and congressional delegations agree that federal cuts to the World Trade Center Health Program will hurt survivors of and first responders to the Sept. 11 terrorist attacks.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, PIX 11

item.title: Senate adopts budget resolution after marathon overnight vote-a-rama
item.description: The Senate adopted a budget resolution Friday intended to serve as a blueprint to deliver the first part of President Trump’s agenda. Senators voted 52-48, mostly along party lines, on the resolution after a marathon overnight voting session. Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.) voted against the measure.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation
